problem_statement: The flight search API is applying the max_price filter with inverted logic: requests like GET /flights/search?max_price=200 return flights priced above 200 instead of flights priced at or below 200.
goal: Correct the flight search filtering so max_price acts as an upper bound and only returns flights with price <= max_price.
acceptance_criteria: GET /flights/search?max_price=200 returns only flights with price less than or equal to 200.
Flights priced above the requested max_price are excluded from results.
Existing search behavior for other filters remains unchanged.
constraints: Smallest likely fix is in backend/app/services/flight_service.py, specifically search_flights.
Validate whether any route/service tests cover the max_price filter and update/add a regression test if needed.
unknowns: Whether the inverted comparison is implemented directly in search_flights or delegated to a helper/query builder.
Whether any downstream schema or route layer transforms the filter value before it reaches the service.
repo_summary: Backend-focused repository with flight search API, service layer, schema/model, and tests. Relevant files likely center on backend/app/services/flight_service.py with surrounding route and test coverage in backend/app/api/routes/flights.py and backend/app/tests/test_flight_service.py.

targeted_symbols.reason: This is the affected function named in the issue; the inverted max_price comparison is most likely implemented here.
Confirms the request parameter mapping for max_price into the service call and rules out route-layer inversion.
Best place to add/confirm a regression test for max_price upper-bound behavior.

localization_rationale: The issue description explicitly names backend/app/services/flight_service.py (search_flights) as the affected file.
The bug is a classic inverted comparison on a numeric upper-bound filter, which is most likely localized to search query construction inside the service layer.
The route file is secondary but relevant only to verify parameter passing; the service and its tests are the smallest implementation surface.
